rationale: The citation is not just a literature-list mention: the citing paper treats Kivetz, Netzer, and Srinivasan (2004) as a key prior demonstration of attraction effects with less stylized stimuli. It then explicitly re-tests that study in Appendix B using closely matched designs and reports null results. These nonreplications are used to support the paper's core claim that attraction effects are less robust and more boundary-limited than commonly asserted.
relation_description: The citing paper directly reevaluates empirical evidence from Kivetz et al. (2004), including a dedicated replication of the Economist subscription decoy task, and finds no attraction effect. It uses this contrast to argue against the prior strength and generality of the original finding.
